title: More Nonsense Limerick 28
content:
There was an old person of Slough,
    Who danced at the end of a bough;
    But they said, "If you sneeze, 
    You might damage the trees,
    You imprudent old person of Slough."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "More Nonsense Limerick 28", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
